2001-11-01.title: A Rare Leader Tries to Save Mexico's Fallen Party
2001-11-01.abstract: Beatriz Paredes has emerged as leader of once-dominant Institutional Revolutionary Party, rare woman at top of Mexican politics; holds party must embrace calls for change to be credible opposition force; is trying to rally both party faithful and potential new supporters; is seen as someone who could draw women and young people back to party; photo (M)

2001-11-01.title: Paul Warnke, 81, a Leading Dove in Vietnam Era, Dies
2001-11-01.abstract: Paul C Warnke, who was highest Pentagon official in Johnson administration to question openly the aims and conduct of Vietnam War and later, as Pres Jimmy Carter's chief arms-control negotiator, sought to trim American and Soviet nuclear stockpiles, dies at 81; photo (L)

2001-11-01.title: World Briefing
2001-11-01.abstract: AFRICA KENYA: DON'T FORGET US, BOMB VICTIMS SAY Kenyans wounded in the 1998 bombing of the American Embassy in Nairobi accused the United States government of failing to compensate them while rushing to the aid of those injured in the Sept. 11 attacks on New York and Washington. The victims insisted on cash payouts and dismissed as paltry the more than $40 million the United States has spent on medical care and reviving businesses.

2001-11-01.title: World Briefing | Europe: Denmark: Premier Calls Quick Election
2001-11-01.abstract: Danish Prime Min Poul Nyrup Rasmussen calls election for Nov 20, saying that waiting for mid-March deadline would force four months of campaigning on country at time of uncertainty brought on by terror attacks of Sept 11 (S)

2001-11-01.title: Protests and Firing of Cabinet Plunge Georgia Into New Crisis
2001-11-01.abstract: Georgia descended further into political crisis today after a police raid on a popular television station set off mass protests and the dismissal of most of the government.

2001-11-02.title: Rebel Chief Flees Raid By Taliban
2001-11-02.abstract: Anti-Taliban leader Hamid Karzai reports from Derawat, Aghanistan, that he is indeed alive and well and that his forces did not sustain any casualties in fighting against Taliban militia in southern part of country; Karzai is considered prime target as he tries to foment anti-Taliban uprising which he hopes will attract major defectors from ruling regime (M)

2001-11-02.title: Step Right Up! On Show in Iran, Sins of Uncle Sam
2001-11-02.abstract: Iran marks 22d anniversary of takeover of US Embassy with special exhibit on grounds of compound, opened to public for first time; features include carnival game involving pounding on devilish face wearing US-flag hat; photos; conservatives use occasion to stoke fresh anti-American sentiment, even as those who once climbed walls mellow and officials take tentative steps toward cooperating with US (M)

2001-11-02.title: Trouble in Deploying Commandos Is Said to Hurt U.S. Air Campaign
2001-11-02.abstract: Accurate US bombing of front-line Taliban forces in northern Afghanistan has been delayed for weeks by difficulty of infiltrating up to 100 elite American commandos; Sec Donald Rumsfeld and other officials cite howling sandstorms, ground fire and coordination problems with opposition forces; US special forces are training and organizing rebel militias and spotting targets for warplanes, but only about two dozen are on ground so far; delays complicate campaign to oust Taliban, get Osama bin Laden and promote resistance in south, where some angry Pashtuns seem to have rallied to Taliban; national security adviser Condoleezza Rice sees no bombing pause for Ramadan, saying enemy has to be pressed to the end; Gen Richard B Myers reports B-52's are having some effect on entrenched Taliban troops around Kabul and Mazar-i-Sharif; he and Rumsfeld say such strikes now account for 80 percent of missions (M)

2001-11-02.title: World Briefing | Africa: Zimbabwe: Economic Outlook Gloomy
2001-11-02.abstract: Zimbabwe Finance Min Simba Makoni, in annual budget speech to parliament, says he expects economy to shrink by 7.3 percent this year (S)

2001-11-02.title: Bin Laden Letter Calls Upon Pakistanis to Defend Islam
2001-11-02.abstract: Western and Arab news organizations receive copies of letter handwritten in Arabic, carrying Osama bin Laden's signature, urging Pakistan's Muslims to defend Islam against what it calls Christian crusade; letter is given relatively little coverage by Arab and Western news programs (M)

2001-11-03.title: Bin Laden's Statement
2001-11-03.abstract: Following is the translated text of Osama Bin Laden's latest statement, as posted on the British Broadcasting Corporation's web site.

2001-11-03.title: Without U.S. Support, Pashtun Rebel Leader Is Finding Few Allies
2001-11-03.abstract: Hamid Karzai, anti-Taliban Pashtun commander now on clandestine mission in southern Afghanistan, must persuade potential allies that US will provide kind of military support that so far has gone largely to Northern Alliance; relatives outside Afghanistan describe efforts by Karzai, who is favorite of some of Afghanistan experts in Washington (M)

2001-11-03.title: U.S. Widens Net to Snare Terror Assets; Expands List
2001-11-03.abstract: Bush administration imposes stringent financial sanctions on Hamas, Hezbollah and 20 other suspected terrorist organizations; move is sign that admistration feels confident enough in Arab countries' support for action against Osama bin Laden to include anti-Israeli groups that have considerable support in Arab world; US has long frozen Hamas and Hezbollah assets in US, but members of Congress have urged stronger action; new list includes other Arab groups, three Colombian groups, Real Irish Republican Army and Basque group ETA; latter are included to show US is not just focusing on Israel and Palestinians; photos of Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah and Hamas founder Ahmed Yassin (M)

2001-11-03.title: Japan Blocks Trip By Foreign Minister
2001-11-03.abstract: japan blocks trip by foreign minister**Japan Foreign Min Makiko Tanaka has shakier hold on her job after ruling opposition legislators join forces to deny her permission to participate in opening of United Nations General Assembly; Prime Min Junichiro Koizumi says he finds vote 'disappointing' (S)

2001-11-03.title: World Briefing | Asia: Indonesia: Ready To Freeze Assets
2001-11-03.abstract: Indonesia will issue decree allowing banks to freeze assets of terrorism suspects as United States has requested (S)

2001-11-04.title: Bin Laden, in a Taped Speech, Says Attacks in Afghanistan Are a War Against Islam
2001-11-04.abstract: Osama bin Laden portrays American military campaign in Afghanistan as war against Islam; his videotaped speech, broadcast by Al Jazeera television network in Qatar, is seen as attempt to play to Arab and Muslim popular opinion; he denounces as infidels Muslim leaders who cooperate with United Nations and refers to its secretary general, Kofi Annan, as 'a criminal'; he praises what he calls 'great strikes' made against New York and Washington on Sept 11, and says US has not produced any evidence for its attacks against Afghanistan; says it is not war against terrorism, but against religion and creed; Bush administration has vehemently denied that campaign against terrorism begun after Sept 11 attacks is directed against Islamic faith; photo (M)

2001-11-04.title: The Temperature's a Lot Warmer but the Mission's the Same: Hunting Down Terrorists
2001-11-04.abstract: American military advisers are in Zamboanga in southern Philippines to pursue terrorist group, Abu Sayyaf, which American officials link to Osama bin Laden; government of Philippines is determined to wipe out Abu Sayyaf, and has found dedicated, well-equipped partner in United States; Lt Cmdr Jeff Davis says Abu Sayyaf is not just Philippine problem; calls it international terrorist group that poses as much of threat to US as to Philippines; nevertheless, suggestion that Abu Sayyaf is Philippine branch of bin Laden's Al Qaeda strikes people in Philippines as faintly bizarre; they say Abu Sayyaf has lost whatever religious fervor it once had; photo (M)

2001-11-04.title: China's Villagers Vote, but Its Party Rules
2001-11-04.abstract: Village elections have become more widespread and in some cases more free in China over last three years, but local Communist Party leaders, appointed from above, retain real power; conflicts between elected committees and party secretaries are on rise; map; photo (M)m

2001-11-04.title: In Japan, Beef Business Sinks in a Sea of Skepticism
2001-11-04.abstract: Japanese consumers abandon beef in response to single case of mad cow disease; beef sales have fallen 50 percent; beef barbecue restaurants are changing their menus and cutting prices; consumers lack faith in government in wake of disclosure that it suppressed European Commision warning that Japan was likely candidate for disease; map; photo (M)

2001-11-04.title: Rumsfeld Visits Russia and Central Asia to Bolster Coalition
2001-11-04.abstract: Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld, beginning trip through Russia and Central Asia aimed at bolstering antiterrorism coalition, meets with Russian Min Sergei B Ivanov and later with Pres Vladimir V Putin; Ivanov says they discussed use of Russian intelligence to support United States military campaign in Afghanistan and arms control; again says Russian troops are not involved in war; sees some merit in Rumsfeld push to abandon Antiballistic Missile Treaty; photo (M)

2001-11-05.title: U.S. MAY GAIN USE OF MORE AIR BASES TO STRIKE TALIBAN
2001-11-05.abstract: Tajikistan and US move toward agreement that could lead to American military strikes against Taliban forces from its territory; Tajikistan would receive millions of dollars in return; Pentagon officials say that if bases are found to be in satisfactory condition, warplanes could use them for bombing raids against Taliban forces in Afghanistan; say air field could be used by helicopter-borne Special Operations forces, and as hub for military supplies being sent to Northern Alliance and relief supplies being dropped to Afghan refugees by US Air Force; US military will also look at bases in Kyrgyzstan and Kazakhstan; Joint Chiefs of Staff chairman, Gen Richard B Myers, says US is rushing supplies to Northern Alliance forces so they can fight through winter; photo; map (M)

2001-11-05.title: Team of American Military Personnel Arrives in Rebel Territory in Afghanistan
2001-11-05.abstract: Senior official in Northern Alliance reports that six-member team of American military personnel has landed in alliance territory north of Kabul to inspect new airstrip and to carry out covert activities; whether sluggish and fractious opposition alliance will actually carry out attack near Kabul is unknown; alliance exercises and arrival of American team suggests effort may be under way to begin attack before winter and Islamic holy month of Ramadan; photo (M)

2001-11-05.title: Rumsfeld Tells India That U.S. Wants to Extend Military Ties
2001-11-05.abstract: The United States is committed to building closer military ties with India, an ally in the global campaign against terrorism, the defense secretary told Indian officials today.

2001-11-05.title: Lull in Tension, Traveling Diplomacy and New Help in the War Zone
2001-11-05.abstract: Overview of day's events in Afghan war front, with Defense Sec Donald Rumsfeld arriving in Pakistan after quick visits to Russia, Tajikistan and Uzbekistan

2001-11-05.title: U.S. Jets Pound Taliban Positions in Heaviest Bombing Yet
2001-11-05.abstract: American jets pound Taliban positions in heaviest bombing yet along country's far northern front, as rebel troops appear to take their first steps toward mounting attack on the ground; dozens of bombs are dropped on single ridge in Kalakata near border with Tajikistan; for all their intensity, bombs are unable to wipe out Taliban postions there; alliance fighter claims Taliban are riding out bombing by ducking into caves in hillsides; photo; map (M)

2001-11-06.title: European Union Brings Peres and Arafat Together Again
2001-11-06.abstract: Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat and Israeli Foreign Min Shimon Peres meet for second time in three days, joining European Union officials for informal gathering in Brussels; European Union, in absence of high-level American engagement in Middle East, is pressing hard to provide nervous Arab world with at least impression of movement in peace process after 13 months of deterioration and bloodshed; photo (M)

2001-11-06.title: World Briefing | Africa: Kenya: Somalia Border Reopened
2001-11-06.abstract: Pres Daniel Arap Moi agrees to open Kenya's 420-mile border with Somalia in recognition of progress in talks between Somali government and opposition warlords; ordered border closed in July, saying illegal arms were entering his country (S)

2001-11-06.title: Sharon Cites Possible Surge In Attacks for Delaying Trip
2001-11-06.abstract: Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon explains decision to postpone upcoming trip to US, saying he needs to remain in Israel to cope with possible surge in attacks as result of new army tactics in confrontation with Palestinians; says army, after withdrawing troops from Qalqilya in West Bank and expected withdrawal from other Palestinian-governed cities, will use other means to combat terrorism (M)

2001-11-06.title: Nicaragua Voters Reject Ortega's Bid for the Presidency
2001-11-06.abstract: Enrique Bolanos, businessman arrested and stripped of property in 1980's for outspoken opposition to Sandinista rule, is elected president of Nicaragua, defeating nemesis, Daniel Ortega, by surprisingly wide margin; captures estimated 53.7 percent of vote, compared with 44.7 percent for Ortega, on strong voter turnout; political analysts say margin of victory, which defied opinion polls showing candidates in statistical tie, resulted from fear of Sandinistas' past and declarations by US officials who doubted Ortega's sincerity in stating that he had moderated views and wanted national reconciliation; photos (M)

2001-11-06.title: Central Banks Poised for a New Round of Rate Cuts
2001-11-06.abstract: LONDON—  As the world's largest economies appear headed into a simultaneous recession, central bankers stand poised this week to deliver a new round of interest-rate relief. But economists are divided on the size of the cuts and on whether they will stimulate a rebound.

2001-11-07.title: World Briefing | Asia: Sri Lanka: Mud Flies Before Election
2001-11-07.abstract: Pres Chandrika Kumaratunga of Sri Lanka accuses her former parlimentary affairs minister, S B Dissanayake, of having offered to kill two critical journalists (S)

2001-11-07.title: Europeans Urge Israelis and Palestinians to Resume Talks
2001-11-07.abstract: European Union urges Israelis and Palestinians to resume talks, without preconditions, to establish lasting cease-fire, ensure security and move toward peace talks on settlement that would establish independent Palestine; Min Shimon Peres and Yasir Arafat hold several meetings in Brussels, Peres reporting he pressed Palestinian leader to crack down on terror attacks; photo; Javier Solana says what matters is that Israelis and Arabs met at all (M)

2001-11-07.title: Afghan Rebels Assert Gains, Saying U.S. Troops Helped
2001-11-07.abstract: Northern Alliance spokesmen say they have captured three districts near approach to strategic city of Mazar-i-Sharif in northern Afghanistan with help of US military advisers; photo; map; Sec Donald Rumsfeld warns US has not confirmed claim; Qudratullah Hurmat says Americans in fight gave orders to commanders, organized troops, photographed Taliban positions and delivered ammunition by helicopter; claims capture of Taliban fighters (M)

2001-11-07.title: World Briefing | Americas: Nicaragua: Carter Wary On Vote Count
2001-11-07.abstract: Former Pres Jimmy Carter, leading observer mission to Nicaragua, urges vigilance as election officials resume counting votes from presidential and legislative elections; Enrique Bolanos defeats Daniel Ortega in presidential vote; Carter photo (S)

2001-11-07.title: U.S. TAKES STEPS TO BOLSTER BLOC FIGHTING TERROR
2001-11-07.abstract: Bush administration takes first steps to expand coalition fighting Taliban and Al Qaeda terrorist network after weeks of deflecting European offers of military forces; acts amid signs of waning popular support overseas for bombing campaign; Pres Bush speaks by video to Central and Eastern European leaders and in Rose Garden with French Pres Jacques Chirac; photos; likens Al Qaeda to fascists who darkened 20th century, saying they show 'same mad, global ambitions' and brutal determination to control every life; also warns that Osama bin Laden seeks biological, chemical and nuclear weapons; Britain has urged enlarging coalition; Germany pledges 3,900 troops who could become first German forces outside Europe since end of World War II; NATO weighs dramatic mission to get food into Afghanistan, joining US effort; Italy offers 1,000 troops and equipment; France also offers commandos and warplanes but does not have base nearby; Pentagon says more than 50,000 National Guardsmen and Reservists have been mobilized; 16 European leaders listening in Warsaw applaud Bush reference to their own past domination by Soviet Union (M)

2001-11-08.title: World Briefing | Asia: Cambodia: 4th Opposition Candidate Killed
2001-11-08.abstract: Opposition candidate for local elections in Cambodia is beaten to death, fourth such murder (S)

2001-11-08.title: The Sleek Concorde Is Once More Carrying Passengers
2001-11-08.abstract: Concorde, world's only supersonic jetliner, resumes service for first time since July 2000 Air France crash; France has treated plane's return, with stronger tires, armor-plated wiring and redesigned fuel tank, as matter of national honor; photo (M)

2001-11-08.title: World Briefing | Americas: Brazil: Not Happy With U.S.
2001-11-08.abstract: Nepal and Bhutan fail to resolve longstanding dispute over refugees; about 100,000 Hindus fled mostly Buddhist Bhutan decade ago and settled in UN-run camps in Nepal (S)

2001-11-08.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Israel: Arab Lawmaker Faces Charges
2001-11-08.abstract: Israeli lawmakers vote to lift parliamentary immunity of Azmi Bishara, Israeli Arab legislator, so he can be prosecuted for remarks urging resistance to Israel, and for arranging Israeli Arab visits to Syria; photo (S)

2001-11-08.title: Sailor Is Feared To Be Lost at Sea From Kitty Hawk
2001-11-08.abstract: American sailor aboard aircraft carrier Kitty Hawk is feared lost at sea after helicopter crew tries unsuccessfully to save him; officials say sailor went overboard at dusk; refuse to cite location of carrier; if confirmed lost, sailor would be fourth American death in war against Taliban and Al Qaeda (S)

2001-11-09.title: World Briefing | Europe: Turkey: Immigration Accord With Greece
2001-11-09.abstract: Turkey and Greece agree that Greece will be able to return illegal immigrants to Turkey; long-sought deal comes as Greece fears new wave of illegal refugees from US bombing campaign in Afghanistan (S)

2001-11-09.title: To the Stranger, a Wild Land, Strangely Awesome
2001-11-09.abstract: Reporter's Notebook describes night sky in Afghanistan, where natural world looms large; stars and moon are very bright without competition from electric lights; cratered landscape is harsh; there is no bridge across Kokcha River so people cross on horseback; elements dictate life in land where rains--and hunger--can force people to move; describes working in mud-brick hut that operates with gas-powered generator and no plumbing; photo (M)

2001-11-09.title: Reporter's Notebook; No Jerry Lewis, but Saudi Telethon Reaches Goal
2001-11-09.abstract: Reporter's Notebook on Saudi Arabia, where recent telethon raised more than $36 million for Afghan people; photos; Saudi government picks careful path between supporting US campaign against terrorism and expressing public's unease about civilian suffering; telethon mirrors government's way of throwing money at nasty problems, and leaving unpleasant details under rug; television Islamic-style, Americans at American International School and lack of jobs for young educated Saudis also discussed (M)

2001-11-09.title: Book and Disk Drives Seen as Evidence in Rebel's Slaying
2001-11-09.abstract: Article discusses investigation in Great Britain of Yasser al-Siri, who has been arrested in connection with killing of Northern Alliance leader Ahmed Shah Massoud; photo (M)

2001-11-09.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: No Ban On Hitting Children
2001-11-09.abstract: British government will not introduce legislation making it illegal for parents to hit children in England and Wales even though Scotland has already announced plans to propose such a law (S)

2001-11-10.title: PAKISTANI LEADER SEEKS  'GESTURES' FOR  BACKING  U.S.
2001-11-10.abstract: Pres Pervez Musharraf says he is visiting United States in search of major gestures from Bush administration to blunt criticism in Pakistan of his strong support for war on terrorism, interview; seeks release of 28 F-16 jets, whose delivery was blocked because of Pakistan's development of nuclear arms; says he is unsure how long bombing campaign can continue in Afghanistan without becoming threat to his government; sees intensity of demonstrations against stance waning; photo (M)

2001-11-10.title: Rebel With Long Career of Picking Fights (and Sides)
2001-11-10.abstract: Article profiles Gen Abdul Rashid Dostum, described as model of modern Uzbek warlord; his forces were instrumental in taking control of Mazar-i-Sharif, important crossroad in northern Afghanistan; photo (M)

2001-11-10.title: IRAN CHIEF REJECTS BIN LADEN MESSAGE
2001-11-10.abstract: Iranian Pres Mohammad Khatami brands as extremist Osama bin Laden's version of Islam, interview; says it does not represent majority of world's Muslims; scores Sept 11 terrorist attacks in United States; says Iran has not been given proof of bin Laden's role in attacks; denies Iran spurs terrorism; scores Israel but suggests Iran might eventually recognize it; photo (M)

2001-11-10.title: Bush Chides Some Members of Coalition for Inaction in War Against Terrorism
2001-11-10.abstract: In preview of speech to United Nations General Assembly, Pres Bush is critical of countries who express sympathy for Sept 11 attack, but fail to take action on behalf of coalition against terrorism; speaking at White House news conference, Bush does not name names as he calls for action; photo (M)

2001-11-10.title: Giovanni Leone, Italy's Ex-President, Dies at 93
2001-11-10.abstract: Former Pres Giovanni Leone of Italy dies at age 93; photo (S)

2001-11-11.title: World Trade Organization Admits China, Amid Doubts
2001-11-11.abstract: China joins World Trade Organization; ceremony in Doha, Qatar, ends 15 years of haggling over whether China's fitful embrace of market economy entitles it to full trading rights of capitalist countries; American and European negotiators in Doha continue to press for trade liberalization while poorer nations demand concessions on trade issues they care about, such as drug patents (M)

2001-11-11.title: Al Qaeda Sites Point to Tests Of Chemicals
2001-11-11.abstract: US identifies sites in Afghanistan that it suspects were involved in Osama bin Laden's efforts to acquire and produce chemical and biological weapons, but none have been bombed since military campaign began; Al Qaeda is believed to have already produced cyanide gas at site in Derunta; other sites are fertilizer plant in Mazar-i-Sharif and anthrax-vaccine plant in Kabul (M)

2001-11-11.title: Rift Grows Among Anti-Taliban Leadership
2001-11-11.abstract: Split appears within political leadership of Northern Alliance that has potential to sap alliance's ability to govern territory it has seized, retard its military operations and delay formation of post-Taliban government; younger, pro-Western, religious moderates are led by foreign minister, Dr Abdullah Abdullah, interior minister, Yunos Qanooni, and Anwari, leader of ethnic Hazara forces; older, religious conservatives, who are more skeptical of West, are led by alliance's president, Burhanuddin Rabbani, and one of its more senior Pashtun leaders, Abdul Rasul Sayyaf; photo (M)

2001-11-11.title: 'No Isolation From Evil,' Bush Declares
2001-11-11.abstract: Excerpts from Pres Bush's speech to United Nations General Assembly on global context of US war against terrorism

2001-11-11.title: Global Warming Impasse Is Broken
2001-11-11.abstract: Final details of Kyoto Protocol emerge from talks in Morocco, after four years of negotiations to curb global warming (M)

2001-11-12.title: Taliban Begin to Pull Out of Kabul as Rebels Move In
2001-11-12.abstract: After a string of successes by the Northern Alliance in Afghanistan, the forces of the Taliban appeared to be retreating from Kabul, the capital, a senior Bush administration official said tonight.

2001-11-12.title: Oil Diplomacy Muddled U.S. Pursuit of bin Laden, New Book Contends
2001-11-12.abstract: Article discusses new book by Jean-Charles Brisard published in France titled Bin Laden: The Forbidden Truth, which asserts that America was unwilling to confront Saudi Arabia over Osama bin Laden and that oil dictates American foreign policy; photo (M)

2001-11-12.title: Equality at Trade Talks: No Country Gets a Vote
2001-11-12.abstract: Reporter's Notebook column on World Trade Organization summit in Doha, Qatar; says that representatives fear they will deliver shock to world economy unless they agree to liberalize trade, but that only shocking thing may be if manage to agree on anything at all; notes trade organization operates by consensus since voting is forbidden, meaning that smallest holdout can in principle sink weightiest agreement; photo (M)

2001-11-12.title: Iraqi Defectors Tell of Kuwaitis in Secret Jail in Baghdad
2001-11-12.abstract: Two Iraqi defectors, veterans of country's intelligence service, say they worked in secret site outside Baghdad where 80 Kuwaitis captured during 1991 war are detained in an underground prison; contradict Iraqi government's longstanding contention that it has not been holding any Kuwaitis; Kuwaiti government has received many reports of people being held, but says they cannot be confirmed (M)

2001-11-12.title: On One Afghan Front Line, the Rebel Tide Surges, Then Flows Back
2001-11-12.abstract: Article describes scene near Chagatai in northern Afghanistan, as front line of fighting teeters forward and back and soldiers advance and retreat in kind; photo (M)

2001-11-13.title: World Briefing | Americas: Mexico: Leftist Wins Race for Governor
2001-11-13.abstract: Lazaro Cardenas, candidate of left-wing Democratic Revolutionary Party, is elected governor of Michoacan state, Mexico, defeating Alfredo Anaya of Institutional Revolutionary Party; photo (S)

2001-11-13.title: Powell Says Muslim Nations Should Be Peacekeepers in Kabul
2001-11-13.abstract: The secretary of state said that Turkey, Bangladesh and Indonesia have offered forces for an operation that would prepare the ground for an interim government.

2001-11-13.title: Bush and Putin Agree to Reduce Stockpile of Nuclear Warheads
2001-11-13.abstract: President Bush and President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia pledged today to cut their nuclear stockpiles by roughly two-thirds over the next decade.

2001-11-13.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: War Criminals Lose Pensions
2001-11-13.abstract: Simon Wiesenthal Center says Germany has stopped paying pensions to 72 World War II veterans who have been accused of or prosecuted for crimes against humanity; additional 11 pensions could be canceled in coming weeks and around 250 other investigations are in progress that could lead to prosecutions for war crimes (S)

2001-11-13.title: Trade Deal Near for Broad Access to Cut-Rate Drugs
2001-11-13.abstract: Trade ministers attending international summit in Doha, Qatar, all but clinch deal that would give developing countries greater access to cut-price drugs to fight epidemics, defusing most inflammatory dispute between rich and poor nations that had threatened to scuttle talks; breakthrough creates sense of momentum as mediators seek consensus on multiyear agenda for global trade liberalization; negotiators warn substantial differences remain to be settled before talks end on November 15; US help in brokering deal, despite strong opposition from multinational pharmaceutical companies, discussed (M)

2001-11-14.title: Bush and Putin Agree to Reduce Stockpile of Nuclear Warheads
2001-11-14.abstract: Pres Bush and Russian Pres Vladimir V Putin separately vow deep cuts in their nuclear stockpiles over next decade that would leave each side with fewer than 2,200 warheads; Putin calls for written accord, move Bush wants to avoid, news conference, Washington; two nations still seem far apart on missile defenses, with Putin apparently resisting United States bid for quick accord to ease ABM Treaty, meeting with Bush; Bush and Putin stress emergence of US and Russia as allies, news conference; Bush pledges to speed through Congress bill to take Russia and other former Soviet republics off list of nations subject to economic sanctions; much of their four-hour meeting focuses on Afghanistan; Putin suggests joint consulations and action on terrorism of last two months are model for new military ties with US and NATO; photos (M)

2001-11-14.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Chechnya Withdrawal Planned, AGain
2001-11-14.abstract: Gen Gennadi Troshev says most Russian tro

2001-11-14.title: World Briefing | Asia: South Korea: Farmers Stage Protest Over W.T.O. Rice Deal
2001-11-14.abstract: Thousands of demonstrate, Seoul, demanding South Korea pull out of accord with World Trade Organization under which it will cut subsidies for home-grown rice; photo (S)

2001-11-14.title: Buenos Aires Journal; Home From Cuba, Soccer Star Kicks Some Shins
2001-11-14.abstract: Argentines are transfixed by bittersweet return of revered soccer great Diego Armando Maradona after two years in Cuba, where he was treated for cocaine abuse; Maradona, 41, overweight and sluggish, plays with national team against international all-stars in exhibition match in his honor; photos (M)

2001-11-14.title: A 3rd Front: War's Spoils
2001-11-14.abstract: Analysis of war in Afghanistan notes United States military, which created conditions for sudden fall of Kabul to Northern Alliance, has little control over alliance, which moved into city despite its repeated promises to wait; notes other US allies in war against terrorism in Afghanistan are also unpredictable; military success leaves US with no clear answers to questions about Afghanistan's future; alliance is now pledging to keep bulk of its forces outside city; has deployed 3,000 security troops across city to bring order, not occupy it (M)

2001-11-15.title: U.S. Flies Aid Workers to Safety in Pakistan
2001-11-15.abstract: Eight Western aid workers, including two Americans, are plucked out of Afghanistan by United States military helicopters and flown to Pakistan; they were arrested and imprisoned by Taliban in Kabul on Aug 5, accused of preaching Christianity; they were put on trial and threatened with death penalty; their trip out of captivity involves International Committee of Red Cross as well as local Pashtun militia leader; details of rescue mission are unclear; spokesman for American Embassy in Islamabad refuses to provide futher details, citing privacy laws; two Americans, Dayna Curry and Heather Mercer, as well as two Australians and four Germans, were working for German-based charity, Shelter Now; Pres Bush hails their safe exit from Afghanistan; photos (M)

2001-11-15.title: Rapid Changes on the Ground Lead the Pentagon to Focus on Counterguerrilla Tactics
2001-11-15.abstract: American officials consider new war plan that would shift from bombing to counterguerilla operations; strategy would emphasize gathering intelligence and hunting leaders of Taliban and Al Qaeda and use coalition troops to secure key areas; effect could result in diminishing airstrikes that coincide with beginning of holy month of Ramadan, without official declaration of reduction in air campaign (M)

2001-11-15.title: World Briefing | Asia: China: Crackdown In Muslim Region
2001-11-15.abstract: Chinese officials in largely Muslim western province of Xinjiang extends country's anticrime crackdown to religious groups that do not have official approval (S)

2001-11-15.title: Food Shipment May Be Start of Something Big
2001-11-15.abstract: Aid arrives in Afghanistan via route through Uzbekistan, which aid workers say, will allow them to get significant shipments where relief is most needed; photo; map (M)

2001-11-15.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Wartime Bomb Near Reichstag
2001-11-15.abstract: Unexploded Russian bomb from World War II is unearthed during construction near Reichstag in Berlin, Germany, forcing temporary shutdown of building's observation deck (S)

2001-11-16.title: A Need for Order, the Smallpox Matter and Oil Prices
2001-11-16.abstract: Overview of events in war on terrorism (M)

2001-11-16.title: Allies Building Force to Keep Order in a Vacuum
2001-11-16.abstract: Western nations rush to assemble international force to keep order in Afghan areas wrested from Taliban; 160 British marines and American Special Operations troops fly to airfield north of Kabul and begin preparing it for shipments of food; American forces seek to make contact with Northern Alliance officers who forward credible claims of capturing some Taliban commanders and at least one high-ranking Al Qaeda leader; prisoners could provide valuable intelligence about location of Osama bin Laden and Mullah Muhammad Omar, chief of Taliban; country appears deeply fractured, with rival warlords filling power vacuum left by departing Taliban and with Mullah Omar vowing to fight on and 'destroy America'; America's allies signal willingness to fill power vacuum; France, Canada, Turkey and Jordan offer to send troops to maintain security in Afghanistan; Bangladesh and Malaysia said to be preparing to contribute as well; sense of urgency stems from fact that Taliban's resistance in northern Afghanistan has collapsed more quickly than expected, raising fears that unstable situation might interfere with delivery of badly needed aid shipments; photo (L)

2001-11-16.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Chechen Rebel Goes On Trial
2001-11-16.abstract: The highest-ranking Chechen guerrilla ever captured by Russian forces went on trial with three other rebels in Dagestan, a southwestern province bordering Chechnya. Salman Raduyev is charged with leading an attack on a Dagestani town in 1996 in which 70 people died and 2,000 were taken hostage. Mr. Raduyev fought mostly in the first Chechen war, in the mid-1990's; he has played little role in the second war, which began in 1999 and continues today.   Michael Wines (NYT)

2001-11-16.title: Before and After Bush and Putin's Banter, No Agreement on Missile Defense
2001-11-16.abstract: President Bush and President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia ended their three-day summit meeting today with warm jokes and a pledge to bring stability to Afghanistan, but no discernible progress on amending the treaty that constrains Washington's plans to test an antimissile system.    After 24 hours on Mr. Bush's ranch here, punctuated by hours of private discussions and a barbecue dinner on Tuesday night that included lessons in how to dance the Cotton-Eyed Joe, the two presidents emerged this morning to visit the local high school.

2001-11-16.title: U.S. Says Top Bin Laden Aide Believed Killed in Airstrike
2001-11-16.abstract: Mohammed Atef, the Al Qaeda terror network's military chief and a close confidant of Osama bin Laden, is believed to have been killed in an American airstrike.

2001-11-17.title: World Briefing | Africa: South Africa: Arrest In Brotherly Deal
2001-11-17.abstract: Police in South Africa arrest Shabir Shaik, brother of Defense Dept's chief acquisitions officer, on charges of improperly obtaining classified documents that helped him win lucrative defense contract; acquisitions officer is found to have conflict of interest in matter (S)

2001-11-17.title: Bogotá Journal; Corruption's Her Story; Colombia Doesn't Like It
2001-11-17.abstract: Ingrid Betancourt, who has written about her fight against corruption in Colombia, is former congresswoman and senator who won widespread support abroad for denouncing corrupt colleagues and graft-ridden administration of former president, Ernesto Samper; Colombian news media is strangely silent about her new book detailing corruption, and although many Colombians blame institutional corruption for country's unending civil unrest, Betancourt's style of in-your-face accusations does not sit well; nor does her airing of country's dirty launder abroad; her persistence has led to death threats against her and her family; photo (M)

2001-11-17.title: Rebels Pause for Ramadan, but Vow Swift Attack on Kunduz
2001-11-17.abstract: Northern Alliance fighters encircling Kunduz, Afghanistan, pause briefly to celebrate start of Ramadan, but commanders say they will be moving, perhaps within 24 hours, to attack city where they have as many as 20,000 Taliban fighters surrounded; photo (M)

2001-11-17.title: Chirac to Visit Algeria in North Africa Tour
2001-11-17.abstract: Pres Jacques Chirac will make first visit to Algeria by French head of state in 12 years as part of North African tour; Chirac, who faces presidential elections in April, is said to be trying to polish his image as international statesman (M)

2001-11-17.title: Taliban Leader Said to Be Yielding Grip on Stronghold to 2 Once-Powerful Supporters
2001-11-17.abstract: The Taliban leader, Mullah Muhammad Omar, a main target of the American war in Afghanistan, has decided to cede control of his political stronghold in Kandahar to two commanders from the Pashtun ethnic group, the Afghan Islamic Press reported tonight.     The retreat would end the seven-year reign of the extremist Islamic group, which gave safe harbor to Osama bin Laden and Al Qaeda.

2001-11-18.title: Alliance Jails Foreign Foes, But Isn't Sure About Origins
2001-11-18.abstract: Of the 75 to 80 prisoners being held at Arg prison here, 5 are from outside Afghanistan. That makes them ''important prisoners,'' said the prison's commander, Haji-Abdul Kareem Qayabatani, adding that ''they are evidence that other countries were involved'' in supporting the Taliban.     No one knows, however, exactly which countries.

2001-11-18.title: Argentina's Provinces Struggle to Stay Afloat
2001-11-18.abstract: Gov. Alfredo Avelín is an eccentric country doctor known for his homespun philosophy, his poetry and a peculiar habit of playfully slapping the faces of visitors to his office.     So perhaps it is not surprising that the other day he broke into a full-throated rendition of the tango classic ''It's a Kid's Dream'' when asked about President Fernando de la Rúa's plans to balance the federal budget to avert a national financial collapse.

2001-11-18.title: Unexpected Guests Warm Hearts in the Frozen North
2001-11-18.abstract: It could have been a short, sweet story: planes get diverted, local people pitch in to help stranded passengers. Polite thank-you letters and gifts follow.    What happened in Newfoundland in one terrifying week in September was all that. But in the next two months, the story continued to grow. Here and in scattered hamlets for miles around, everyone has a part of it to tell -- how half a dozen or so isolated communities have been embraced by strangers who dropped from the sky and changed their lives.

2001-11-18.title: U.S. to Press Afghan Rebels Not to Form Government
2001-11-18.abstract: The Bush administration said today that it would apply intense pressure on the Northern Alliance, newly in control of Kabul and much of Afghanistan, not to create a government on its own but to fulfill its promise to let the United Nations put together a broad coalition to rule the country.    At the same time, after a meeting of the National Security Council conducted from President Bush's ranch here this morning, the administration also vowed to vigorously prosecute the war against remaining Taliban holdouts. It pledged, as well, to refocus its efforts on crushing Al Qaeda, the terrorist network, and its surviving leaders, who apparently include Osama bin Laden.

2001-11-18.title: RETRACING A TRAIL TO SEPT. 11 PLOT
2001-11-18.abstract: Shortly after Sept. 11, when the United States sent out urgent inquiries for leads on the suspected hijackers, German officials quickly traced three of the ringleaders to an apartment in this affluent northern port.    It was an important break. But the names and address -- Marienstrasse 54 -- suddenly seemed familiar, German investigators say, and then they realized why: in 1998 and 1999, they had those same men and that same apartment under surveillance because of suspected links to an operative for Osama bin Laden.

2001-11-19.title: The Corrupt and Brutal Reclaim Afghan Thrones, Evoking Chaos of Somalia
2001-11-19.abstract: Galaxy of corrupt warlords, who tore Afghanistan apart in early 1990's before being vanquished by Taliban, regain control of strategic cities they fled six years ago and are poised to exercise power in ways they always have; rapid return will make forming broad-based Afghan government Bush administration says it wants to help forge much more difficult (M)

2001-11-19.title: Kosovo Winner Wants Independence
2001-11-19.abstract: Ibrahim Rugova, whose party easily wins elections in Kosovo and is expected to become its first president, announces that his first priority is to seek independence of province, which is still technically part of Serbia (S)

2001-11-19.title: Pakistan Fears 3,000 Pro-Taliban Troops Have Slipped In
2001-11-19.abstract: Pakistani intelligence officials, addressing subject of mounting concern, estimate 3,000 or more Tailban and Al Qaeda fighters slipped over border into country's tribal areas in week since fall of Kabul and could add to potentially volatile mix in semiautonomous region; fear they could mount antigovernment operations inside Pakistan or cross back into Afghanistan to carry out hit-and-run raids; some tribal leaders remain sympathetic to Taliban and are willing to conceal them out of longstanding loyalties; photo (M)

2001-11-19.title: A Deadly Siege At Last Won Mazar-i-Sharif
2001-11-19.abstract: Northern Alliance commander says at least 300 Taliban soldiers died in fighting when his troops laid siege to Taliban barracks at Mazar-i-Sharif; says most of those housed in barracks were foreign fighters; alliance fought for two weeks to take city, advancing from three directions and using 600 fighters on horseback as well as standard fighting units; United Nations spokeswoman raises fears that captured men were slain; Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld calls such reports 'unsubstantiated and sensational' (M)

2001-11-19.title: U.S. Publicly Accusing 5 Countries of Violating Germ-Weapons Treaty
2001-11-19.abstract: United States will accuse North Korea, Iraq, Iran, Libya and Syria of developing germ weapons in violation of treaty they ratified banning such weapons; Bush administration officials say they suspect additional countries are violating treaty in secret, including some that are friendly with US, but administration is not ready to identify them; accusations will be formally made in Geneva by John R Bolton, under secretary of state, at international conference aimed at strengthening compliance with and enforcement of treaty; public nature of accusations is departure; allegations are not specific, nor is source of any evidence provided; they are intended to deflect criticism of Bush administration from those who say Washington has undermined treaty by rejecting agreement last summer that was meant to strengthen compliance by establishing inspection system (M)

2001-11-20.title: South Africa Sees Turmoil Dividing Ruling Party
2001-11-20.abstract: The governing African National Congress hooted with delight when South Africa's leading white party collapsed last month, declaring that the last of its political opponents had been swept away.    But in recent weeks, officials have privately acknowledged that the most serious threat to A.N.C.'s political dominance is alive and growing. The threat comes not from this nation's white minority, but from dissatisfied blacks within the party ranks.

2001-11-20.title: Serb Turnout in Kosovo Vote Seen as an Encouraging Step
2001-11-20.abstract: Kosovo's Serbs turned out in much greater numbers than originally thought in elections on Saturday, showing that they see a future despite the Albanian domination of their province and securing a real level of power in a new provincial assembly, Western officials said today.    Most Serbs appear to have voted just before polls closed, and after dark so they would not be seen by community leaders opposed to taking part in the vote for a 120-seat assembly. The elections were devised by the international officials who have ruled Kosovo since the war over it ended in June 1999 and NATO troops arrived here.

2001-11-20.title: 4 Foreign Journalists Are Shot And Possibly Killed in Ambush
2001-11-20.abstract: Four foreign journalists are reportedly kidnapped and shot dead by armed gunmen on Afghan highway halfway from Jalalabad to Kabul; fifth man, translator, is reported missing; witness says gunmen claimed to be Taliban; journalists' bodies have not been found; they are Maria Grazia Cutuli, reporter for Italian daily Corriere della Sera, Julio Fuentes from El Mundo in Madrid, and Harry Burton, Australian television cameraman, and Aziz Haidari, Afghan-born photogrpher, both working for Reuters; journalists were in second and third cars of eight-car convoy; their cars were stopped at gunpoint by men clad in style favored by Taliban; photos (M)

2001-11-20.title: Conflicting Tales Paint Blurry Picture of Siege
2001-11-20.abstract: Article describe siege of Taliban stronghold of Mazar-i-Sharif and its capture by forces of Northern Alliance (M)

2001-11-20.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Security Fears At U.S. Embassy
2001-11-20.abstract: The United States is considering closing its original embassy building on Novinsky Boulevard and moving all operations deeper within the embassy's square-block compound because of security concerns, a senior diplomatic official said. Much of the building was vacated after Sept. 11 and many workers moved to a new embassy building, in the center of the compound, which was recently completed after almost 15 years of construction. The new embassy was completely rebuilt after security experts found in the mid-1980's that its framework was riddled with listening devices.   Michael Wines (NYT)

2001-11-21.title: An Investigation in Egypt Illustrates Al Qaeda's Web
2001-11-21.abstract: Data from probe for 1999 trial in Egypt of Islamic Jihad details scope and ruthlessness of terrorist group, which merged with Al Qaeda in 1998, vastly enhancing Osama's bin Laden's reach and organizational ability; network two groups developed ranges across world, with Egyptians providing tactical support by forging travel documents, transferring money and arranging communications; merger shifted Islamic Jihad's focus from overthrowing Egyptian government to attacking American interests and seems to have increased Egyptians' sense of purpose; Dr Ayman al-Zawahiri, Jihad's leader and bin Laden's second in command, reportedly was with bin Laden in Afghanistan earlier in November; photos (M)

2001-11-21.title: Fearful City Wonders Whether Taliban Will Leave Quietly or Fight
2001-11-21.abstract: Travelers from Kandahar, Afghanistan, say air of fear and suspense hangs over city as Taliban leader Mullah Muhammad Omar seems to be maneuvering toward his exit; say dwindling number of remaining civilians are rattled by American bombs and by uncertainty over whether Taliban will slip quietly out of city or stay and fight; say intense talks are under way among Taliban hierarchy and between some senior Taliban and opposition commanders (M)

2001-11-21.title: OBITUARY : Gene Fenn, 90, Photographer
2001-11-21.abstract: PARIS—  Gene Fenn, 90, a fashion photographer for Harper's Bazaar and other popular women's magazines, died Nov. 14 in Paris.

2001-11-21.title: Foreign Militants in Kunduz Seek Safe Passage to Pakistan
2001-11-21.abstract: The foreign militants who are trapped inside Kunduz have asked to leave Afghanistan through a protected corridor and travel to Pakistan, a senior Northern Alliance official said today.

2001-11-21.title: Barak Testifies on Israeli Arab Violence
2001-11-21.abstract: Ehud Barak, the former Israeli prime minister, told a state inquiry commission today that he was caught by surprise by the wave of violent protests by Israeli Arabs that erupted after the outbreak of the Palestinian uprising last year.    ''I did not expect those type of events'' Mr. Barak, said, referring to the Israeli Arab protests in October 2000 in which 13 people were killed by police gunfire. ''There was no concrete warning of such an eruption from any intelligence agency. The power, scope and intensity of the violence could not have been expected.''

2001-11-22.title: Taliban Will Rise Again, Aide to Leader Promises
2001-11-22.abstract: Taliban leadership vows not to give up stronghold in southern Afghanistan or spiritual home of Kandahar, ruling out talks with Northern Alliance or other opposition groups on future national government; spokesman, Mullah Omar's private secretary Tayab Agha, warns of return to anarchy and warlordism; photo at news conference (M)

2001-11-22.title: No TV, No Chess, No Kites: Taliban's Code, From A to Z
2001-11-22.abstract: Afghan cooking-oil seller named Hefozullah, arrested in Herat for keeping shop open during Friday prayers, turns out to be last of several thousand tailors, bakers, shoe polishers and confectioners detained by Taliban religious police before they flee city in advance of Northern Alliance; left behind are registers and people who tell of bureaucracy largely indifferent and inept, except in its ruthless enforcement of Mullah Muhammad Omar's harsh Islamic penal code; punishments are spelled out for women who leave home unveiled or alone, for those who fly pigeons or fly kites and for possession of 'unclean things' from pork to photographs to televisions; photos (M)

2001-11-22.title: English Pot Smokers' Pub May Prove a Model
2001-11-22.abstract: Dutch Experience cafe in northern English town of Stockport hosts marijuana smoking and could be thin end of wedge in Britain, where government is signaling it may ease laws on use of soft drugs in name of creating workable drug policy; photos; government says police spend inordinate amount of time dealing with minor drug offenses, with 65 percent of 120,000 drug-related arrests last year for marijuana possession; Stockport police raided cafe day it opened in September but did not act again until cafe began attracting publicity (M)

2001-11-22.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: New Mad Cow Case
2001-11-22.abstract: Second case of mad cow disease is confirmed in Japan, on Hokkaido (S)

2001-11-23.title: 5 Palestinian Boys Die in Blast in Gaza
2001-11-23.abstract: Five Palestinian boys, cousins ranging in age from 6 to 14, were killed when a powerful explosive detonated beneath them near an Israeli settlement in the Gaza Strip. Palestinians said the boys had been killed by an unexploded tank shell, a remnant of recent fighting, but the Israeli authorities said that a Palestinian explosive might be to blame.    Article, Page A3.

2001-11-23.title: Fierce Fighting Erupts Near Kunduz, Despite Surrender Deal
2001-11-23.abstract: Heavy fighting breaks out between Taliban and Northern Alliance troops along front lines outside Kunduz as senior alliance leader says Taliban officials have agreed to surrender encircled city; thousands of Taliban troops and foreign soldiers are trapped in city; fierce fighting suggests that Taliban leaders who struck surrender deal might not speak for all rank-and-file soldiers, or even for other commanders; as fighting rages, several Taliban soldiers cross front lines and switch sides, lending air of chaos to scene; peace deal supposedly worked out by Gen Abdul Rashid Dostum, longtime Uzbek warlord, seems fraught with questions; photos (M)

2001-11-23.title: World Briefing | Europe: Scotland: New Leader For Parliament
2001-11-23.abstract: Jack McConnell, leader of Scottish Labor Party, is elected first minister of Scottish Parliament to replace Henry McLeish, who stepped down in scandal (S)

2001-11-23.title: World Briefing | Africa: Namibia: Threat To Shoot Dismays Rights Group
2001-11-23.abstract: Namibia's minister of defense, Erkki Nghimtina, is condemned by National Society of Human Rights for threatening to shoot anyone found to be collaborating with rebels from neighboring Angola (S)

2001-11-23.title: World Briefing | Asia: Kazakhstan: President Fires Reform Advocates
2001-11-23.abstract: Pres Nursultan Nazarbayev of Kazakhstan dismisses six high government officials who had joined group calling for democratic political reforms; dismissals were demanded by Prime Min Kasymhomart Tokayev, who threatened to resign, saying would-be reformers are seeking to enrich themselves (S)

2001-11-24.title: Taliban Holdouts, Europe's Reservations and Anthrax Frustrations
2001-11-24.abstract: Overview of day's events in war on terrorism, fighting in Afghanistan and anthrax attacks

2001-11-24.title: PAKISTANIS AGAIN SAID TO EVACUATE ALLIES OF TALIBAN
2001-11-24.abstract: Northern Alliance soldiers in Afghanistan report Pakistani airplanes have again flown into encircled city of Kunduz to evacuate Pakistanis who had been fighting with Taliban; photo; map; sightings are not confirmed and US officials are evasive on subject; US is indebted to Pakistan for helping with war but also wants foreign fighters captured or killed; surrender of Kunduz is yet unclear; Taliban forces regroup elsewhere, forming strong pockets of resistance in east, including Jalalabad vicinity (M)

2001-11-24.title: In Kabul, Happiness for Sale
2001-11-24.abstract: Photo of balloon vendors riding to market in Kabul, city throwing off Taliban constrictionsó

2001-11-24.title: Citing Some Progress, U.N. Panel on Torture Urges Israel to Take More Steps
2001-11-24.abstract: UN Committee Against Torture says that while Israel has made some progress in ending practices it says amount to torture, government should install 'effective' complaint and investigation mechanisms; report also cites abuses by Indonesian military and Ukraine's alleged use of torture in jails(M)

2001-11-24.title: Medellín Journal; A Coffee Icon Rides His Mule Off Into the Sunset
2001-11-24.abstract: Carlos Sanchez, aka Juan Valdez, is being retired after thirty years because plummeting coffee prices are forcing Colombian growers' group to cut back on famous ad campaign; map (M)

2001-11-25.title: Taliban Foes Say Kunduz Is Theirs
2001-11-25.abstract: Northern Alliance commanders said tonight that their troops had captured Kunduz, overrunning the last Taliban stronghold in northern Afghanistan.

2001-11-25.title: Wolves Wander Into Germany, Aiding a Resettlement Effort
2001-11-25.abstract: Sighting of wolf pack in nature preserve along Germany's border with Poland gives new impetus to effort of Society for the Protection of Wolves, founded in Germany in 1991 partly to create conditions to resettle animals, which were eradicated from German soil 140 years earlier; pack may have migrated from northwestern Poland or Carpathian Mountains; society's president Peter Blanche comments (M)

2001-11-25.title: As Alliance Deals With a Warlord, Taliban Fighters Slip Away
2001-11-25.abstract: Ghullan Muhammad, warlord of collection of villages in Maidan Shahr, Afghanistan, surrenders to, joins or makes deal with North Alliance; just which it is still is not clear after one day of frantic negotiations and marshaling of forces; hard-core Taliban fighters and unknown number of Islamic zealots slip away during negotiations; they could be headed for Taliban stronghold of Kandahar, or they could be disappearing into mountains, perhaps to fight another day as guerrillas; possibility that Taliban fighters will be allowed to fade into hills, with potential to re-emerge and destabilize Afghanistan, is worrying to Western diplomats trying to reconstruct country; photo (M)

2001-11-25.title: Kabul's New Rulers Lament a Leader Who Died Too Soon
2001-11-25.abstract: Afghanistan's new rulers lament loss of Northern Alliance's legendary guerrilla commander Ahmed Shah Massoud, who was assassinated on Sept 9; Dr Abdullah Abdullah, Northern Alliance foreign minister and one of best friends of Massoud, says Massoud might have saved nation from its tumult; photos (M)

2001-11-25.title: Australia Wants More People, but Without More Immigration
2001-11-25.abstract: Australia, which is as large as US minus Alaska, but which has only 19.3 million people compared with more than 280 million in United States, wants more inviting policies and programs for both migration and parenthood to counter long decline in national birthrate, and to avoid demographic black hole of zero population growth; birth rate of 1.7 births per woman is well below 2.1 'replacement rate' last reached in 1975; similar problem is emerging in political and economic agendas of developed nations elsewhere, particularly in Western Europe and Japan; UN Population Fund report forecasts if currrent trends continue, population of Europe will fall from current 723.6 million to 603.3 million by 2050, and Japan's population of 127.3 million will fall to 109.2 million (M)

2001-11-26.title: TALIBAN FOES SAY KUNDUZ IS THEIRS
2001-11-26.abstract: Northern Alliance commanders report capture of city of Kunduz, last Taliban stronghold in northern Afghanistan: deputy defense minister, Atiqullah Baryalai, says Taliban troops have fled west to uncertain fate toward army of another alliance general, Abdul Rashid Dostum; photo (M)

2001-11-26.title: Where Thousands Of Drought Refugees Wait for Food or Death
2001-11-26.abstract: At least 150,000 Afghans live in refugee camp in Maslakh that has population of major city but none of its services; inhabitants were pushed from their homes, not by war but by drought heading into its fourth year in three provinces in northwestern part of country; people in Maslakh wait for food, for bricks to build homes, and for sick children to die; graveyards are growing every day, result of illness, hunger and cold; photos (M)

2001-11-26.title: Communist Effort to Prosecute Czech Priest Raises Ghosts of Past
2001-11-26.abstract: Czech Republic Communist Party complaint against Rev Vojtech Vlastimil Protivinsky, who urged parishioners to vote against local Communist candidates, has reawakened bitterness of many persecuted under past Communist rule; Pres Vaclav Havel has blocked prosecution and local prosecutor who began proceedings has resigned; Protivinsky cites need to oppose ideas that are assault on basic human rights; analysts score Communists for using instruments of democracy against priest who disagrees with them (M)

2001-11-26.title: Nature Made The Perfect Hiding Place
2001-11-26.abstract: Afghanistan's varied landscape and geology make it ideal for caves, which are used as hideouts by Taliban and Al Qaeda terrorist network (S)

2001-11-26.title: Film Critics With a Keen Eye for Violence
2001-11-26.abstract: Hundreds of Afghan teenage boys go to movies in Kabul, something they could not do for past six years under Taliban rule; owner of cinema, who was once jailed by Taliban for three weeks, says he hid films in his home for last six years, as did several other theater owners he knows; photo (M)

2001-11-27.title: A Deathly Peace Settles on Kunduz's Streets
2001-11-27.abstract: Liberation of Kunduz, last big garrison of Taliban soldiers in northern Afghanistan by forces of Northern Alliance, described; fate of many foreign Taliban fighters remains unclear; many surrendered, but others vanished and may have left aboard flights villagers saw operating from local airport two weeks before city fell; photos (M)

2001-11-27.title: World Briefing | Europe: Northern Ireland: A Peaceful Walk To School
2001-11-27.abstract: Catholic children walk to North Belfast school peaceably for first time in three months after Protestants end protests that forced them to seek protection of riot squad police officers and British troops; photo (S)

2001-11-27.title: British Defense Secretary Says Troops for Afghanistan Are Being Taken Off High Alert
2001-11-27.abstract: Britain's Defense Sec Geoff Hoon says most of 6,400 British troops, placed on 48-hour alert for action in Afghanistan, will have readiness downgraded to week; says 400 troops from parachute regiment and air assault brigade will remain on two-day standby; reports four soldiers were wounded in fighting in Afghanistan; photo (M)

2001-11-27.title: AIDS Crisis Leaves Africa's Oldest Ways at a Loss
2001-11-27.abstract: Article in series about HIV epidemic in South Africa describes crucial role of sangomas, or traditional healers, in health care; polls say 60 percent of all South Africans consult sangomas; traditional doctors know their patients come to them only after seeing sangoma; Martha Shokwakhe Mtshali, pre-eminent sangoma, admits that one illness that stumps her is AIDS; says if she knew what was in Western AIDS drugs, she might be able to find herbal equivalent; she and her colleagues say they are not hostile to Western medicine; Mtshali seeks working partnership with doctors; because South Africans place so much trust in sangomas, hospitals sometimes 'deputize' them for tasks like monitoring patients who need to take daily drugs; photo; map (M)

2001-11-27.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Egypt: Concern Over Monitored Web Sites
2001-11-27.abstract: Human rights advocates in Egypt express concern that police are monitoring Web sites; cite arrest of Shohdy Naguib after he posted poem condemning Egyptian leaders (S)

2001-11-28.title: World Briefing | Asia: South Korea: Shots Exchanged In DMZ
2001-11-28.abstract: North Korean soldier fires few bullets at South Korean outpost in demilitarized zone; South Korean forces return fire; incident is first of its kind in over three years in zone; map (S)

2001-11-28.title: Envoy Forges Bonds and Reaps Benefits
2001-11-28.abstract: Career sketch of retired Gen Antÿ

2001-11-28.title: U.S. Sees Limited Mission In Postwar Afghanistan
2001-11-28.abstract: Bush administration official says United States seeks to avoid future military obligations in Afghanstan once mission to defeat Taliban and destroy Al Qaeda terrorist network is over; says US expects to be 'partner' in shaping nation's political and economic future, but will not take part in international security force charged with bringing stability to nation (M)

2001-11-28.title: From Jihad To Thoughts Of Studying Computers
2001-11-28.abstract: Interview with Mullah Amirjan Selabe, who commanded 30 Taliban soldiers in Kabul before fleeing to Pakistan month ago; he cites effort to change his way of life; is one of countless Taliban soldiers or commanders who have disappeared by melding selves into Pakistan's Afghan communities; many are transforming their appearance to survive, but not necessarily renouncing their beliefs (M)

2001-11-28.title: World Briefing | Africa: Somalia: The 'Forgotten Emergency'
2001-11-28.abstract: United Nations calls for increased aid to Somalia (S)

2001-11-29.title: Rare Condoms, Deadly Odds For Truck-Stop Prostitutes
2001-11-29.abstract: Truckers from all over southern Africa have sex with prostitutes in Ladysmith, in rural KwaZulu-Natal province of South Africa; it is assumed that is one reason HIV spread so rapidly in province; prostitutes working truck-stops make clear how hard it would have been to stem spread, even if government made concerted effort, which it has not; photo; map (M)

2001-11-29.title: America's War on Terror Ends Years of Indifference to Region : U.S. Woos Southeast Asian Allies
2001-11-29.abstract: SINGAPORE—  After years of seeming indifference, the United States is again courting Southeast Asian countries — this time in its search for allies in the global war against terrorism.

2001-11-29.title: Relations at Last Normal, Vietnam Signs U.S. Trade Pact
2001-11-29.abstract: Vietnam joins United States in approving far-reaching trade agreement that throws open its economy and brings end to final chapter of war; Vu Khoan, Vietnam's minister of trade, says with trade accord, process of normalizing relations between Vietnam and US is fully realized; new agreement is expected to take effect at start of new year; agreement commits Vietnam to series of far-reaching reforms over next several years, setting stage for development of fully free market in Vietnam; American tariffs on Vietnamese goods will be cut to 4 percent from as high as 40 percent, removing Vietnam from company of small number of unfavored states; World Bank estimates that lower tariffs could eventually double amount of Vietnam's exports to US, which currently is $800 million a year; photo (M)

2001-11-29.title: U.S. Envoy Meets Arafat and Asks for End to Violence
2001-11-29.abstract: Gen Anthony C Zinni, Bush administration's new envoy, begins complex task of trying to broker new cease-fire between Israel and Palestinians; scope of his mission and of his authority remain unclear to Israelis and Palestinians, who have expressed doubts about possibility of durable truce after 14 months of bitter conflict; Zinni meets in Ramallah with Yasir Arafat, who says peace is his 'strategic choice'; shortly later, Palestinian gunmen again open fire from town of Beit Jala on Jewish area of Jerusalem, Gilo, that they regard as illegal settlement; Israeli forces return fire; photo (M)

2001-11-29.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Protest Against New Terror Law
2001-11-29.abstract: Schools and businesses close in Srinagar, summer capital of Kashmir, to protest new Indian antiterror measure that was invoked for first time to seal house in Srinagar where suspected Islamic militants are hiding (S)

2001-11-30.title: World Briefing | Europe: Norway: New Theory On Norse God
2001-11-30.abstract: Anthropologist Thor Heyerdahl says his excavations i

2001-11-30.title: Northern Alliance Refuses to Submit Candidates for Interim Government
2001-11-30.abstract: Negotiations on a future government for Afghanistan stalled badly today, after the leader of the Northern Alliance refused to authorize his delegation to submit a list of proposed candidates.

2001-11-30.title: North Korea Threatens U.S. Over Demand for Arms Inspections
2001-11-30.abstract: North Korea threatens 'countermeasures' against US because Bush administration insists on raising questions about its human rights record and religion, and demanding inspection of weapons of mass destruction; threat increases fears of complete breakdown of peace talks engineered by Pres Kim Dae Jung over last year and half (M)

2001-11-30.title: South African Victims Face Rapists and H.I.V.
2001-11-30.abstract: Article in series, Death and Denial, exploring aspects of HIV epidemic in South Africa, describes plight of rape victims; country has one of world's highest rape rates; in rural KwaZulu/Natal province, there is 40 percent chance that young male rapist is HIV positive; public health system does not provide antiretroviral drug therapy for people with HIV, so infection means virtual certainty of death for many rape victims; by law, accused rapist could refuse blood test; this means that even if drugs were available, young women often would not get them in time; photos; map (L)

2001-11-30.title: Many Eager to Help, but Few Are Chosen
2001-11-30.abstract: America's military allies in war on terror are encamped at MacDill Air Force Base in Florida, in so-called Coalition Village; they are outsiders in what some diplomats and military officials say remains essentially all-American fight in Afghanistan; about 35 countries have offered to send aircraft, ships or solders to help hunt down Taliban and Al Qaeda leaders; as more than 1,000 American marines pour into base near Kandahar, some coalition partners wonder whether US really wants their offerings of help afte all; Gen Tommy R Franks, head of military operation, says their help will be used when time is right; photo (M)
